factors.summary: The company's revenue mix is shifting towards higher-margin segments in the Oil & Gas and Industrial & Infrastructure sectors, driven by a shift from lower-margin construction activities to higher-margin engineering activities. evidence.text: The current year improvement is predominantly due to increased contributions from certain upstream projects that were completed or progressing to completion during 2014 and a shift in the mix of work from lower margin construction activities to higher margin engineering activities.

The Oil & Gas segment experienced higher segment profit margin that was partially due to a shift in the mix of work from lower margin construction activities to higher margin engineering activities. This shift corresponds to an increase in the volume of project execution activities for projects that are in the earlier stages of the project life cycle compared to the prior years.

factors.summary: The company's backlog orders are growing in the Oil & Gas and Power segments, while they are shrinking in the Industrial & Infrastructure and Government segments. The overall consolidated backlog increased to $42.5 billion in 2014 from $34.9 billion in 2013, driven by significant new awards in the Oil & Gas and Government sectors. However, specific segment backlogs show variability, with the Oil & Gas segment's backlog decreasing from $17.2 billion in 2012 to $10.5 billion in 2013, and further decreasing to $7.3 billion in 2014. The Government segment's backlog also decreased from $1.0 billion in 2012 to $2.4 billion in 2013, and then to $4.7 billion in 2014, indicating a mixed trend across different segments.

evidence.text: The higher ending backlog as of December 31, 2014 when compared to the prior years is reflective of the higher levels of new award activity during 2014.
